title: Improvement Proposal
trigger: after capability-gap-analysis or component-watch produces actionable findings
description: Draft a structured improvement proposal from gap analysis or component watch findings. Submit for human approval. Never self-modify production.

proposals.resolved_by: Brandon directive 2026-07-17 — keep advancing, do not wait idle
proposals.resolution: Option A: AtomicFileStore (conservative default). Capped at 50 drafts; oldest evicted on overflow. Accepts input.type or input.artifactType; stores content/body. Graceful fallback if store unavailable. Retention/read-back remain future work (no artifact.draft.read handler in v2).
proposals.title: Artifact Draft Write — Storage Backend Design
proposals.summary: artifact.draft.write currently returns a draft ID without persisting any content. Three candidate backends: (1) AtomicFileStore keyed by artifactId — simple, already available, no disk-flood protection; (2) capped local FS directory (e.g. ~/.zao/private/artifacts/, rolling 50-file cap) — matches artifact metaphor, human-readable; (3) Zocuments — proper version history, structured, but adds a dependency. Questions for Zaal: preferred backend? max artifact size? retention policy? approval required to read back artifacts?
design_questions: Which storage backend? (AtomicFileStore / local FS directory / Zocuments)
Retention cap (max number of drafts, or TTL)?
Should artifact.draft.read be a paired handler, or is read-back out of scope for v2?
proposals.security_note: Drafts must never be published without the approval gate. artifact.draft.write stays draft-only regardless of backend.

proposals.resolved_by: Brandon directive 2026-07-17 — keep advancing, do not wait idle
proposals.resolution: Option A: Named-preset lookup in handler. WORKOUT_PRESETS table maps workoutScope/workout string → {toolId, inputs, maxRounds}. Presets: scheduled-session (memory.read, 2 rounds), field-test (state.local.read, 3 rounds), tool-workout (receipt.local.query, 3 rounds), mastery-check (cowork.fetch-projects, 1 round). Unknown scope defaults to scheduled-session. Output: {completed, workout, toolId, passed, rounds, timestamp}.
proposals.title: ToolGym Workout Run — Input Shape Decision
proposals.summary: toolgym.workout.run is a stub because loop manifests pass {workout:'field-test'} (a named preset string) while ToolGymAdapter.runWorkout() expects {toolId, inputs, maxRounds}. Three options: (A) named-preset lookup table in handler maps workout name → {toolId, inputs, maxRounds} — keeps simple string in manifests, adds a maintenance table; (B) update loop manifests to pass structured {toolId, inputs, maxRounds} directly — cleaner contract, requires editing tool-workout.manifest.json + mastery-receipt.manifest.json + field-test.manifest.json; (C) accept both shapes — most flexible, doubles handler complexity. All options are safe local-only changes with no external calls or Pi credentials needed.
design_questions: Which input shape? (A: named-preset lookup in handler / B: structured inputs in manifests / C: accept both)
If Option A: should the preset table live in the handler file or a separate config JSON (e.g. loops/toolgym-presets.json)?
Which named workout presets are needed for v2? (e.g. field-test, tool-workout, mastery-check)
Should workout output include per-round results, or only a final pass/fail summary?
proposals.security_note: toolgym.workout.run must never auto-install packages or modify handler files. All workout steps are bounded, local, read/invoke only.
